close_reason: Completed: added themes/sources/CANONICAL_UPSTREAMS.md decision table with repo+file+variant+pinned ref+pinning strategy for tokyo-night/gruvbox/everforest/osaka-jade, plus future-theme source selection and normalization rules; linked from themes/sources/SCHEMA.md and themes/README.md.
description: Decide and record one canonical upstream source per theme (repo/file/ref/variant), plus normalization policy into internal palette keys. Clarify when Omarchy is canonical vs when original theme upstream is canonical.
notes:
Context captured on 2026-03-03 (America/Montreal):

Recommended canonical source per current theme:
- tokyo-night: folke/tokyonight.nvim | variant=night | reference file=extras/ghostty/tokyonight_night
- gruvbox: morhetz/gruvbox | variant=dark-hard | reference file=colors/gruvbox.vim
- everforest: sainnhe/everforest | variant=dark-medium | reference files=palette.md and autoload/everforest.vim
- osaka-jade: basecamp/omarchy | variant=dark | reference file=themes/osaka-jade/colors.toml

Policy recommendation:
- Use per-theme upstream canonical source (not Omarchy-for-all)
- Pin each theme source to repo+file+ref(tag/commit)+variant
- Normalize into internal palette keys, then generate all app artifacts
- Use overrides only for app-specific deviations

Key findings:
- Omarchy does not currently expose a per-theme canonical upstream mapping field in themes metadata/docs; treat Omarchy as canonical set itself unless theme-specific upstream is chosen.
- Local drift spotted in current repo:
  - themes/themes.json tokyo-night has several accent values not present in current tokyonight upstream main
  - everforest bg mapping differs from canonical medium ordering (bg1/bg2)

Upstream repos verified (HTTP 200 on 2026-03-03):
- https://github.com/folke/tokyonight.nvim
- https://github.com/morhetz/gruvbox
- https://github.com/sainnhe/everforest
- https://github.com/basecamp/omarchy
title: Define canonical upstream sources + variant pinning

close_reason: Implemented a more fault-tolerant AeroSpace on-focus-changed callback and verified reload; documented that Raycast app shortcuts bypass AeroSpace bindings and AutoRaise third-party switcher warping is inherently less reliable.
description: Analyze current dotfiles configuration for AutoRaise, AeroSpace, and Raycast. Determine whether AeroSpace can center the cursor on focus/app switch, identify why cursor warping is unreliable with Raycast app shortcuts, and propose or implement a more reliable fix.
notes: Root cause analysis: Raycast app shortcuts bypass AeroSpace bindings, so any cursor movement tied only to bindings will not run. AutoRaise docs explicitly warn mouse warp is less reliable with third-party task switchers even with altTaskSwitcher=true. Added a more fault-tolerant AeroSpace on-focus-changed callback to home/office/laptop configs using window-lazy-center with monitor-lazy-center fallback so AeroSpace can recenter after external focus changes it observes.
title: Investigate unreliable cursor centering with AutoRaise + AeroSpace + Raycast app switching

description: Current experiments showed AeroSpace on-focus-changed callbacks cannot distinguish manual mouse/hover focus from keyboard-driven app/workspace switches. That makes callback-based cursor centering fundamentally wrong for the desired behavior: center on Raycast/cmd-tab style switches, but never steal the pointer during manual mouse movement. Investigate a source-aware approach instead: likely remove generic AeroSpace focus-centering callbacks, keep logging, and evaluate targeted mechanisms such as AutoRaise alt-task-switcher warp for cmd-tab, Raycast-specific wrapper scripts/hotkeys, or another tool that can identify keyboard/app-switch initiated focus changes. Capture the need to stop workspace 9 -> 1 -> 5 bounce regressions and preserve no-warp behavior for actual mouse movement.
title: Replace generic AeroSpace focus callback with source-aware cursor centering

description:
Goal: Add Chromium browser theming support for the existing theme pipeline, starting with Vantasteez, while preserving the canonical source model.

Current state:
- No Chromium/Chrome integration exists in themes/scripts/theme_build.py or themes/.local/bin/theme-set.
- Canonical source for Vantasteez is themes/sources/vantasteez.toml.
- Generated artifacts currently target meta/, configs/, themes.json, wallpapers/.

Requirements:
1) Keep source-of-truth model: edit only themes/sources/*.toml; generated artifacts are managed output.
2) Implement Chromium theme generation from canonical palette (Vantasteez first, but design should support all themes).
3) Decide and document artifact format (e.g., extension-manifest-based theme package metadata and/or browser-consumable JSON), with rationale.
4) Integrate into generator workflow and tests:
   - Extend themes/scripts/theme_build.py in a non-breaking way.
   - Add/adjust tests in themes/tests/test_theme_build.py.
5) Integrate into runtime apply path if feasible:
   - Update themes/.local/bin/theme-set with a safe Chromium apply step (or document manual apply if fully automated apply is not practical).
6) Update docs:
   - themes/README.md
   - themes/AGENTS.md

Suggested implementation checklist for next session:
- Research/confirm practical Chromium theme apply mechanism on macOS (automated vs manual constraints).
- Add generation target(s) under themes/configs/<theme>/ for Chromium artifacts.
- Wire generator output cleanup for stale files, consistent with existing managed artifacts behavior.
- Add validation and tests for schema/override keys if introducing new overrides.* target.
- Run full validation pipeline:
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --check
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-meta
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-themes-json
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-configs
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-wallpapers
  python3 -m unittest themes/tests/test_theme_build.py

Acceptance criteria:
- Chromium artifact(s) generated from canonical palette for Vantasteez.
- No manual edits needed outside canonical source + generators.
- Tests pass and docs describe usage and limitations clearly.
title: Add Chromium theme pipeline for Vantasteez

close_reason: Committed fix f31c7a6: make ghostty no-folding in installer and docs to prevent theme-set writes from dirtying repo.
description: theme-set writes custom Ghostty theme files to ~/.config/ghostty/themes. If ghostty is stowed with folder folding, ~/.config/ghostty points into the repo and theme switching creates untracked files (e.g. ghostty/.config/ghostty/themes/Vantablack). Make ghostty a no-folding package and repair local setup.
title: Prevent Ghostty theme-set from dirtying repo via Stow folding
